NKJV.1: And Jesus answered and spoke to them again by parables and said:
NKJV.2: “The kingdom of heaven is like a certain king who arranged a marriage for his son,
NKJV.3: and sent out his servants to call those who were invited to the wedding; and they were not willing to come.
NKJV.4: Again, he sent out other servants, saying, ‘Tell those who are invited, “See, I have prepared my dinner; my oxen and fatted cattle are killed, and all things are ready. Come to the wedding.” ’
NKJV.5: But they made light of it and went their ways, one to his own farm, another to his business.
NKJV.6: And the rest seized his servants, treated them spitefully, and killed them.
NKJV.7: But when the king heard about it, he was furious. And he sent out his armies, destroyed those murderers, and burned up their city.
NKJV.8: Then he said to his servants, ‘The wedding is ready, but those who were invited were not  worthy.
NKJV.9: Therefore go into the highways, and as many as you find, invite to the wedding.’
NKJV.10: So those servants went out into the highways and gathered together all whom they found, both bad and good. And the wedding hall was filled with guests.
NKJV.11: “But when the king came in to see the guests, he saw a man there  who did not have on a wedding garment.
NKJV.12: So he said to him, ‘Friend, how did you come in here without a wedding garment?’ And he was  speechless.
NKJV.13: Then the king said to the servants, ‘Bind him hand and foot, take him away, and cast him into outer darkness; there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’
NKJV.14: “For many are called, but few are chosen.”
NKJV.15: Then the Pharisees went and plotted how they might entangle Him in His talk.
NKJV.16: And they sent to Him their disciples with the Herodians, saying, “Teacher, we know that You are true, and teach the way of God in truth; nor do You care about anyone, for You do not regard the person of men.
NKJV.17: Tell us, therefore, what do You think? Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?”
NKJV.18: But Jesus perceived their wickedness, and said, “Why do you test Me, you hypocrites?
NKJV.19: Show Me the tax money.” So they brought Him a denarius.
NKJV.20: And He said to them, “Whose image and inscription is this?”
NKJV.21: They said to Him, “Caesar’s.” And He said to them, “Render therefore to Caesar the things that are  Caesar’s, and to God the things that are  God’s.”
NKJV.22: When they had heard these words, they marveled, and left Him and went their way.
NKJV.23: The same day the Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to Him and asked Him,
NKJV.24: saying: “Teacher, Moses said that if a man dies, having no children, his brother shall marry his wife and raise up offspring for his brother.
NKJV.25: Now there were with us seven brothers. The first died after he had married, and having no offspring, left his wife to his brother.
NKJV.26: Likewise the second also, and the third, even to the seventh.
NKJV.27: Last of all the woman died also.
NKJV.28: Therefore, in the resurrection, whose wife of the seven will she be? For they all had her.”
NKJV.29: Jesus answered and said to them, “You are mistaken,  not knowing the Scriptures nor the power of God.
NKJV.30: For in the resurrection they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but  are like angels of God in heaven.
NKJV.31: But concerning the resurrection of the dead, have you not read what was spoken to you by God, saying,
NKJV.32: ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’? God is not the God of the dead, but of the living.”
NKJV.33: And when the multitudes heard this, they were astonished at His teaching.
NKJV.34: But when the Pharisees heard that He had silenced the Sadducees, they gathered together.
NKJV.35: Then one of them, a lawyer, asked Him a question, testing Him, and saying,
NKJV.36: “Teacher, which is the great commandment in the law?”
NKJV.37: Jesus said to him, “‘You shall love the LORD your God with all your heart, with all your soul, and with all your mind.’
NKJV.38: This is the first and great commandment.
NKJV.39: And the second is like it: ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’
NKJV.40: On these two commandments hang all the Law and the Prophets.”
NKJV.41: While the Pharisees were gathered together, Jesus asked them,
NKJV.42: saying, “What do you think about the Christ? Whose Son is He?” They said to Him, “The Son of David.”
NKJV.43: He said to them, “How then does David in the Spirit call Him ‘Lord,’ saying:
NKJV.44: ‘The  LORD said to my Lord, “Sit at My right hand, Till I make Your enemies Your footstool” ’?
NKJV.45: If David then calls Him ‘Lord,’ how is He his Son?”
NKJV.46: And no one was able to answer Him a word, nor from that day on did anyone dare question Him anymore.

ESV.1: And again Jesus spoke to them in parables, saying,
ESV.2: “The kingdom of heaven may be compared to a king who gave a wedding feast for his son,
ESV.3: and sent his servants  to call those who were invited to the wedding feast, but they would not come.
ESV.4: Again he sent other servants, saying, ‘Tell those who are invited, “See, I have prepared my dinner, my oxen and my fat calves have been slaughtered, and everything is ready. Come to the wedding feast.”’
ESV.5: But they paid no attention and went off, one to his farm, another to his business,
ESV.6: while the rest seized his servants, treated them shamefully, and killed them.
ESV.7: The king was angry, and he sent his troops and destroyed those murderers and burned their city.
ESV.8: Then he said to his servants, ‘The wedding feast is ready, but those invited were not worthy.
ESV.9: Go therefore to the main roads and invite to the wedding feast as many as you find.’
ESV.10: And those servants went out into the roads and gathered all whom they found, both bad and good. So the wedding hall was filled with guests.
ESV.11: “But when the king came in to look at the guests, he saw there a man who had no wedding garment.
ESV.12: And he said to him, ‘Friend, how did you get in here without a wedding garment?’ And he was speechless.
ESV.13: Then the king said to the attendants, ‘Bind him hand and foot and cast him into the outer darkness. In that place there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’
ESV.14: For many are called, but few are chosen.”
ESV.15: Then the Pharisees went and plotted how to entangle him in his words.
ESV.16: And they sent their disciples to him, along with the Herodians, saying, “Teacher, we know that you are true and teach the way of God truthfully, and you do not care about anyone's opinion, for you are not swayed by appearances.
ESV.17: Tell us, then, what you think. Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?”
ESV.18: But Jesus, aware of their malice, said, “Why put me to the test, you hypocrites?
ESV.19: Show me the coin for the tax.” And they brought him a denarius.
ESV.20: And Jesus said to them, “Whose likeness and inscription is this?”
ESV.21: They said, “Caesar's.” Then he said to them,  “Therefore render to Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's.”
ESV.22: When they heard it, they marveled. And they left him and went away.
ESV.23: The same day Sadducees came to him, who say that there is no resurrection, and they asked him a question,
ESV.24: saying, “Teacher, Moses said, ‘If a man dies having no children, his brother must marry the widow and raise up offspring for his brother.’
ESV.25: Now there were seven brothers among us. The first married and died, and having no offspring left his wife to his brother.
ESV.26: So too the second and third, down to the seventh.
ESV.27: After them all, the woman died.
ESV.28: In the resurrection, therefore, of the seven, whose wife will she be? For they all had her.”
ESV.29: But Jesus answered them, “You are wrong, because you know neither the Scriptures nor the power of God.
ESV.30: For in the resurrection they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.
ESV.31: And as for the resurrection of the dead, have you not read what was said to you by God:
ESV.32: ‘I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’? He is not God of the dead, but of the living.”
ESV.33: And when the crowd heard it, they were astonished at his teaching.
ESV.34: But when the Pharisees heard that he had silenced the Sadducees, they gathered together.
ESV.35: And one of them, a lawyer, asked him a question to test him.

ESV.39: And a second is like it: You shall love your neighbor as yourself.

NLT.1: Jesus also told them other parables. He said,
NLT.2: “The Kingdom of Heaven can be illustrated by the story of a king who prepared a great wedding feast for his son.
NLT.3: When the banquet was ready, he sent his servants to notify those who were invited. But they all refused to come!
NLT.4: “So he sent other servants to tell them, ‘The feast has been prepared. The bulls and fattened cattle have been killed, and everything is ready. Come to the banquet!’
NLT.5: But the guests he had invited ignored them and went their own way, one to his farm, another to his business.
NLT.6: Others seized his messengers and insulted them and killed them.
NLT.7: “The king was furious, and he sent out his army to destroy the murderers and burn their town.
NLT.8: And he said to his servants, ‘The wedding feast is ready, and the guests I invited aren’t worthy of the honor.
NLT.9: Now go out to the street corners and invite everyone you see.’
NLT.10: So the servants brought in everyone they could find, good and bad alike, and the banquet hall was filled with guests.
NLT.11: “But when the king came in to meet the guests, he noticed a man who wasn’t wearing the proper clothes for a wedding.
NLT.12: ‘Friend,’ he asked, ‘how is it that you are here without wedding clothes?’ But the man had no reply.
NLT.13: Then the king said to his aides, ‘Bind his hands and feet and throw him into the outer darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’
NLT.14: “For many are called, but few are chosen.”
NLT.15: Then the Pharisees met together to plot how to trap Jesus into saying something for which he could be arrested.
NLT.16: They sent some of their disciples, along with the supporters of Herod, to meet with him. “Teacher,” they said, “we know how honest you are. You teach the way of God truthfully. You are impartial and don’t play favorites.
NLT.17: Now tell us what you think about this: Is it right to pay taxes to Caesar or not?”
NLT.18: But Jesus knew their evil motives. “You hypocrites!” he said. “Why are you trying to trap me?
NLT.19: Here, show me the coin used for the tax.” When they handed him a Roman coin,
NLT.20: he asked, “Whose picture and title are stamped on it?”
NLT.21: “Caesar’s,” they replied. “Well, then,” he said, “give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar, and give to God what belongs to God.”
NLT.22: His reply amazed them, and they went away.
NLT.23: That same day Jesus was approached by some Sadducees — religious leaders who say there is no resurrection from the dead. They posed this question:
NLT.24: “Teacher, Moses said, ‘If a man dies without children, his brother should marry the widow and have a child who will carry on the brother’s name.’
NLT.25: Well, suppose there were seven brothers. The oldest one married and then died without children, so his brother married the widow.
NLT.26: But the second brother also died, and the third brother married her. This continued with all seven of them.
NLT.27: Last of all, the woman also died.
NLT.28: So tell us, whose wife will she be in the resurrection? For all seven were married to her.”
NLT.29: Jesus replied, “Your mistake is that you don’t know the Scriptures, and you don’t know the power of God.
NLT.30: For when the dead rise, they will neither marry nor be given in marriage. In this respect they will be like the angels in heaven.
NLT.31: “But now, as to whether there will be a resurrection of the dead — haven’t you ever read about this in the Scriptures? Long after Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob had died, God said,
NLT.32: ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.’ So he is the God of the living, not the dead.”
NLT.33: When the crowds heard him, they were astounded at his teaching.
NLT.34: But when the Pharisees heard that he had silenced the Sadducees with his reply, they met together to question him again.
NLT.35: One of them, an expert in religious law, tried to trap him with this question:
NLT.36: “Teacher, which is the most important commandment in the law of Moses?”
NLT.37: Jesus replied, “‘You must love the LORD your God with all your heart, all your soul, and all your mind.’
NLT.38: This is the first and greatest commandment.
NLT.39: A second is equally important: ‘Love your neighbor as yourself.’
NLT.40: The entire law and all the demands of the prophets are based on these two commandments.”
NLT.41: Then, surrounded by the Pharisees, Jesus asked them a question:
NLT.42: “What do you think about the Messiah? Whose son is he?” They replied, “He is the son of David.”
NLT.43: Jesus responded, “Then why does David, speaking under the inspiration of the Spirit, call the Messiah ‘my Lord’? For David said,
NLT.44:
‘The LORD said to my Lord,
Sit in the place of honor at my right hand
until I humble your enemies beneath your feet.’
NLT.45: Since David called the Messiah ‘my Lord,’ how can the Messiah be his son?”
NLT.46: No one could answer him. And after that, no one dared to ask him any more questions.

AMP.1: Jesus spoke to them again in parables, saying,
AMP.2: "The kingdom of heaven may be compared to a king who gave a wedding feast for his son.
AMP.3: And he sent his servants to call those who had [previously] been invited to the wedding feast, but they refused to come.
AMP.4: Then he sent out some other servants, saying, 'Tell those who have been invited, "Look, I have prepared my dinner; my oxen and fattened calves are butchered and everything is ready; come to the wedding feast."'
AMP.5: But they paid no attention [they disregarded the invitation, treating it with contempt] and went away, one to his farm, another to his business.
AMP.6: The rest [of the invited guests] seized his servants and mistreated them [insulting and humiliating them] and killed them.
AMP.7: The king was enraged [when he heard this], and sent his soldiers and destroyed those murderers and burned their city.
AMP.8: Then he said to his servants, 'The wedding [feast] is ready, but those who were invited were not worthy.
AMP.9: So go to the main highways that lead out of the city, and invite to the wedding feast as many as you find.'
AMP.10: Those servants went out into the streets and gathered together all the people they could find, both bad and good; so the wedding hall was filled with dinner guests [sitting at the banquet table].
AMP.11: "But when the king came in to see the dinner guests, he saw a man there who was not dressed [appropriately] in wedding clothes,
AMP.12: and he said, 'Friend, how did you come in here without wearing the wedding clothes [that were provided for you]?' And the man was speechless and without excuse.
AMP.13: Then the king said to the attendants, 'Tie him hand and foot, and throw him into the darkness outside; in that place there will be weeping [over sorrow and pain] and grinding of teeth [over distress and anger].'
AMP.14: For many are called (invited, summoned), but few are chosen."
AMP.15: Then the Pharisees went and conspired together plotting how to trap Him by [distorting] what He said.
AMP.16: They sent their disciples to Him, along with the Herodians, saying, "Teacher, we know that You are sincere and that You teach the way of God truthfully, without concerning Yourself about [what] anyone [thinks or says of Your teachings]; for You are impartial and do not seek anyone's favor [and You treat all people alike, regardless of status].
AMP.17: Tell us then, what do You think? Is it permissible [according to Jewish law and tradition] to pay a poll-tax to Caesar, or not?"
AMP.18: But Jesus, aware of their malice, asked, "Why are you testing Me, you hypocrites?
AMP.19: Show me the coin used for the poll-tax." And they brought Him a denarius [a day's wage].
AMP.20: And Jesus said to them, "Whose likeness and inscription is this?"
AMP.21: They said, "[ The Emperor Tiberius] Caesar's." Then He said to them, "Then pay to Caesar the things that are Caesar's; and to God the things that are God's."
AMP.22: When they heard this, they were caught off guard, and they left Him and went away.
AMP.23: On that day some Sadducees, who say that there is no resurrection [of the dead], came to Him and asked Him a question,
AMP.24: saying, "Teacher, Moses said, '<e>If a man dies, leaving no children, his brother as next of kin shall</e><e>marry his widow, and raise children for his brother</e>.'
AMP.25: Now there were seven brothers among us; the first married and died, and having no children left his wife to his brother.
AMP.26: The second also [died childless], and the third, down to the seventh.
AMP.27: Last of all, the woman died.
AMP.28: So in the resurrection, whose wife of the seven will she be? For they all had married her."
AMP.29: But Jesus replied to them, "You are all wrong because you know neither the Scriptures [which teach the resurrection] nor the power of God [for He is able to raise the dead].
AMP.30: For in the resurrection neither do men marry nor are women given in marriage, but they are like angels in heaven [who do not marry nor produce children].
AMP.31: But as to the resurrection of the dead—have you not read [in the Scripture] what God said to you:
AMP.32: '<e>I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob</e>'? He is not the God of the dead, but of the living."
AMP.33: When the crowds heard this, they were astonished at His teaching.
AMP.34: Now when the Pharisees heard that He had silenced (muzzled) the Sadducees, they gathered together.
AMP.35: One of them, a lawyer [an expert in Mosaic Law], asked Jesus a question, to test Him:
AMP.36: "Teacher, which is the greatest commandment in the Law?"
AMP.37: And Jesus replied to him, " '<e>You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your mind</e>.'
AMP.38: This is the first and greatest commandment.
AMP.39: The second is like it, 'You shall love your neighbor as yourself [that is, unselfishly seek the best or higher good for others].'
AMP.40: The whole Law and the [writings of the] Prophets depend on these two commandments."
AMP.41: Now while the Pharisees were [still] gathered together, Jesus asked them a question:
AMP.42: "What do you [Pharisees] think of the Christ (the Messiah, the Anointed)? Whose Son is He?" They said to Him, "The son of David."

AMP.44:
'The Lord (the Father) said to my Lord (the Son, the Messiah),

Until I put Your enemies under Your feet"'?

MSG.10: The servants went out on the streets and rounded up everyone they laid eyes on, good and bad, regardless. And so the banquet was on—every place filled.
MSG.11: "When the king entered and looked over the scene, he spotted a man who wasn't properly dressed.
MSG.12: He said to him, 'Friend, how dare you come in here looking like that!' The man was speechless.
MSG.13: Then the king told his servants, 'Get him out of here—fast. Tie him up and ship him to hell. And make sure he doesn't get back in.'
MSG.14: "That's what I mean when I say, 'Many get invited; only a few make it.' "
MSG.15: That's when the Pharisees plotted a way to trap him into saying something damaging.
MSG.16: They sent their disciples, with a few of Herod's followers mixed in, to ask, "Teacher, we know you have integrity, teach the way of God accurately, are indifferent to popular opinion, and don't pander to your students.
MSG.17: So tell us honestly: Is it right to pay taxes to Caesar or not?"
MSG.18: Jesus knew they were up to no good. He said, "Why are you playing these games with me? Why are you trying to trap me?
MSG.19: Do you have a coin? Let me see it." They handed him a silver piece.
MSG.20: "This engraving—who does it look like? And whose name is on it?"
MSG.21: They said, "Caesar." "Then give Caesar what is his, and give God what is his."
MSG.22: The Pharisees were speechless. They went off shaking their heads.
MSG.23: That same day, Sadducees approached him. This is the party that denies any possibility of resurrection.
MSG.24: They asked, "Teacher, Moses said that if a man dies childless, his brother is obligated to marry his widow and get her with child.
MSG.25: Here's a case where there were seven brothers. The first brother married and died, leaving no child, and his wife passed to his brother.
MSG.26: The second brother also left her childless, then the third—and on and on, all seven.
MSG.28: Now here's our question: At the resurrection, whose wife is she? She was a wife to each of them."
MSG.29: Jesus answered, "You're off base on two counts: You don't know your Bibles, and you don't know how God works.
MSG.30: At the resurrection we're beyond marriage. As with the angels, all our ecstasies and intimacies then will be with God.
MSG.31: And regarding your speculation on whether the dead are raised or not, don't you read your Bibles? The grammar is clear: God says,
MSG.32: 'I am—not was—the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, the God of Jacob.' The living God defines himself not as the God of dead men, but of the living."
MSG.33: Hearing this exchange the crowd was much impressed.
MSG.34: When the Pharisees heard how he had bested the Sadducees, they gathered their forces for an assault.
MSG.35: One of their religion scholars spoke for them, posing a question they hoped would show him up:
MSG.36: "Teacher, which command in God's Law is the most important?"
MSG.37: Jesus said, "'Love the Lord your God with all your passion and prayer and intelligence.'
MSG.38: This is the most important, the first on any list.
MSG.39: But there is a second to set alongside it: 'Love others as well as you love yourself.'
MSG.40: These two commands are pegs; everything in God's Law and the Prophets hangs from them."
MSG.41: As the Pharisees were regrouping, Jesus caught them off balance with his own test question:
MSG.42: "What do you think about the Christ? Whose son is he?" They said, "David's son."
MSG.43: Jesus replied, "Well, if the Christ is David's son, how do you explain that David, under inspiration, named Christ his 'Master'?
MSG.44: God said to my Master, "Sit here at my right hand until I make your enemies your footstool."
MSG.45: "Now if David calls him 'Master,' how can he at the same time be his son?"
MSG.46: That stumped them, literalists that they were. Unwilling to risk losing face again in one of these public verbal exchanges, they quit asking questions for good.

NIV.1:
The Parable of the Wedding Banquet
Jesus spoke to them again in parables, saying:
NIV.2: “The kingdom of heaven is like a king who prepared a wedding banquet for his son.
NIV.3: He sent his servants to those who had been invited to the banquet to tell them to come, but they refused to come.
NIV.4: “Then he sent some more servants and said, ‘Tell those who have been invited that I have prepared my dinner: My oxen and fattened cattle have been butchered, and everything is ready. Come to the wedding banquet.’
NIV.5: “But they paid no attention and went off—one to his field, another to his business.
NIV.6: The rest seized his servants, mistreated them and killed them.
NIV.7: The king was enraged. He sent his army and destroyed those murderers and burned their city.
NIV.8: “Then he said to his servants, ‘The wedding banquet is ready, but those I invited did not deserve to come.
NIV.9: Go to the street corners and invite to the banquet anyone you find.’
NIV.10: So the servants went out into the streets and gathered all the people they could find, both good and bad, and the wedding hall was filled with guests.
NIV.11: “But when the king came in to see the guests, he noticed a man there who was not wearing wedding clothes.
NIV.12: ‘Friend,’ he asked, ‘how did you get in here without wedding clothes?’ The man was speechless.
NIV.13: “Then the king told the attendants, ‘Tie him hand and foot, and throw him outside, into the darkness, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’
NIV.14: “For many are invited, but few are chosen.”
NIV.15:
Paying Taxes to Caesar
Then the Pharisees went out and laid plans to trap him in his words.
NIV.16: They sent their disciples to him along with the Herodians. “Teacher,” they said, “we know you are a man of integrity and that you teach the way of God in accordance with the truth. You aren’t swayed by men, because you pay no attention to who they are.
NIV.17: Tell us then, what is your opinion? Is it right to pay taxes to Caesar or not?”
NIV.18: But Jesus, knowing their evil intent, said, “You hypocrites, why are you trying to trap me?
NIV.19: Show me the coin used for paying the tax.” They brought him a denarius,
NIV.20: and he asked them, “Whose portrait is this? And whose inscription?”
NIV.21:
“Caesar’s,” they replied.
Then he said to them, “Give to Caesar what is Caesar’s, and to God what is God’s.”
NIV.22: When they heard this, they were amazed. So they left him and went away.
NIV.23:
Marriage at the Resurrection
That same day the Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to him with a question.
NIV.24: “Teacher,” they said, “Moses told us that if a man dies without having children, his brother must marry the widow and have children for him.
NIV.25: Now there were seven brothers among us. The first one married and died, and since he had no children, he left his wife to his brother.
NIV.26: The same thing happened to the second and third brother, right on down to the seventh.
NIV.28: Now then, at the resurrection, whose wife will she be of the seven, since all of them were married to her?”
NIV.29: Jesus replied, “You are in error because you do not know the Scriptures or the power of God.
NIV.30: At the resurrection people will neither marry nor be given in marriage; they will be like the angels in heaven.
NIV.31: But about the resurrection of the dead—have you not read what God said to you,
NIV.32: ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’? He is not the God of the dead but of the living.”
NIV.33: When the crowds heard this, they were astonished at his teaching.
NIV.34:
The Greatest Commandment
Hearing that Jesus had silenced the Sadducees, the Pharisees got together.
NIV.35: One of them, an expert in the law, tested him with this question:
NIV.36: “Teacher, which is the greatest commandment in the Law?”
NIV.37: Jesus replied: “‘Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind.’
NIV.38: This is the first and greatest commandment.
NIV.39: And the second is like it: ‘Love your neighbor as yourself.’
NIV.40: All the Law and the Prophets hang on these two commandments.”
NIV.41:
Whose Son Is the Christ?
While the Pharisees were gathered together, Jesus asked them,
NIV.42:
“What do you think about the Christ? Whose son is he?”
“The son of David,” they replied.
NIV.43: He said to them, “How is it then that David, speaking by the Spirit, calls him ‘Lord’? For he says,
NIV.44:
“‘The Lord said to my Lord:
“Sit at my right hand
until I put your enemies
under your feet.”’
NIV.45: If then David calls him ‘Lord,’ how can he be his son?”
NIV.46: No one could say a word in reply, and from that day on no one dared to ask him any more questions.
